{"step_by_step_thinking": "The protein ABCG2, also known as Breast Cancer Resistance Protein (BCRP), is an ATP-binding cassette (ABC) transporter. ABC transporters are characterized as active efflux mechanisms that utilize ATP to transport molecules across cellular membranes against concentration gradients. These transporters are integral membrane proteins, meaning they span the lipid bilayer and are embedded within the cell membrane. Multiple documents confirm that ABCG2 is an efflux pump found in tissues such as the liver, kidney, central nervous system, and gastrointestinal tract. For example, Document [3] states that ABCG2 is an efflux pump that transports anticancer drugs like methotrexate and topotecan and is encoded by the ABCG2 gene. Document [14] classifies ABC transporters as active efflux mechanisms that utilize ATP to extrude drugs against concentration gradients, which inherently requires them to be transmembrane proteins. Since ABCG2 is a member of the ABC transporter family and functions as a membrane-bound efflux pump, it must be transmembrane. Therefore, based on the biological classification and functional description of ABCG2, it is a transmembrane protein.", "answer_choice": "A"}
